reasoning: In the first half of 2024, the EU imported 25 bcm of Russian gas, averaging 12.5 bcm per quarter (with Q2 2024 specifically at 12.4 bcm). This occurred while the Ukraine transit route was fully operational, supplying roughly 16 bcm annually (or ~4 bcm per quarter). The cessation of the Ukraine transit route in January 2025 drastically limits the import avenues for Russian gas, capping physical supply routes to the TurkStream pipeline (max capacity to EU is ~15.75 bcm/yr or under 4 bcm/quarter) and LNG imports (which run around 18-20 bcm/yr or roughly 5 bcm/quarter). This puts the maximum physically sustainable import limit of Russian gas at around 9-10 bcm per quarter without Ukraine transit. Reaching 15 bcm in any quarter in 2025 would require an impossible 50% increase over the remaining physical pipeline/LNG capacity and run entirely contrary to the EU's established REPowerEU phase-out goals. The crowd forecast of 1% accurately gauges this structural impossibility. Obeying the system rules bounding probabilities, my final estimate is 5%.
